record.field: LiteLLM is an open-source AI gateway and Python SDK that exposes 100+ LLM providers through a single, OpenAI-compatible API.

LiteLLM is an open-source AI gateway and Python SDK that exposes 100+ LLM providers through a single, OpenAI-compatible API.

It lets teams call models from OpenAI, Anthropic, Azure, AWS Bedrock, Google Vertex AI, Cohere, Hugging Face, vLLM and many more without rewriting code per provider. Deployed as a self-hosted proxy server, it acts as a central control plane for LLM access: issuing virtual API keys, tracking spend, enforcing rate limits and guardrails, and load-balancing or failing over across deployments. It is built for platform and ML-infrastructure teams that need to govern, observe and standardize how applications consume language models.

Key features
  • Unified OpenAI-compatible interface to 100+ LLM providers (chat, embeddings, images, audio, rerank, batches)
  • Virtual keys with per-project and per-user cost tracking, budgets and rate limiting
  • Router with retries, fallbacks and load balancing across multiple model deployments
  • Configurable guardrails, caching and request/response logging per project
  • Observability callbacks (Langfuse, MLflow, Lunary and others) and an admin dashboard
  • MCP tool gateway and A2A agent gateway support; available as Python SDK or standalone proxy
Best for

Platform, MLOps and engineering teams that want a single governed entry point for many LLM providers, with centralized authentication, cost control, observability and resilience across models.

field.name: tag_ids
field.eval: [Command.set([ref('agz_infra_suite.tag_self_hosted'), ref('agz_infra_suite.tag_ai'), ref('agz_infra_suite.tag_proxy'), ref('agz_infra_suite.tag_dev_tools'), ref('agz_infra_suite.tag_monitoring'), ref('agz_infra_suite.tag_security'), ref('agz_infra_suite.tag_llm')])]

record.field: #!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Dump consistente de PostgreSQL (servicio en marcha) → $BACKUP_DATA_DIR docker compose exec -T litellm-db sh -c 'pg_dump -U "$POSTGRES_USER" -d "$POSTGRES_DB" --no-owner -Fc' \ > "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump" echo "pg_dump OK ($(wc -c < "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump") bytes)"

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Levanta solo la DB, espera readiness y restaura docker compose up -d litellm-db for i in $(seq 1 30); do docker compose exec -T litellm-db sh -c 'pg_isready -U "$POSTGRES_USER"' && break sleep 2 done docker compose exec -T litellm-db sh -c 'pg_restore -U "$POSTGRES_USER" -d "$POSTGRES_DB" --clean --if-exists --no-owner' \ < "$RESTORE_DATA_DIR/db.dump" # Verificación: la DB no quedó vacía (sh -c con dobles comillas; $VAR del contenedor con \$) N=$(docker compose exec -T litellm-db sh -c "psql -U \$POSTGRES_USER -d \$POSTGRES_DB -tAc \"SELECT count(*) FROM information_schema.tables WHERE table_schema='public'\"") echo "tablas restauradas: $N" test "$N" -gt 0
